channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: G20 summit calls for more aid to Gaza and an end to the war in Ukraine
item.description: Leaders of the world’s 20 major economies called for a global pact to combat hunger, more aid to war-torn Gaza and an end to hostilities in the Mideast and Ukraine, issuing a joint declaration that was heavy on generalities but short of details on how to accomplish those goals.
item.dc:creator: Mauricio Savarese, Eléonore Hughes And David Biller, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Biden's Ukraine missile move will have 'some impact': Retired major
item.description: Lyons questioned the timing of the decision, saying, “I’m not sure why we didn’t allow this two or three years ago.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Moscow warns the US over allowing Ukraine to hit Russian soil with longer-range weapons
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — The Kremlin warned Monday that President Joe Biden’s decision to let Ukraine strike targets inside Russia with U.S.-supplied longer-range missiles adds “fuel to the fire” of the war and would escalate international tensions even higher.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV and SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Japanese poet Shuntaro Tanikawa, master of modern free verse, dies at 92
item.description: Shuntaro Tanikawa, who pioneered modern Japanese poetry, poignant but conversational in its divergence from haiku and other traditions, has died.
item.dc:creator: Yuri Kageyama, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Greece to repay chunk of bailout debt early and touts recovery during anger over cost of living
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — Greece will make an early repayment of 5 billion euros ($5.3 billion) in bailout-era debt in 2025, Prime Minister Kyriakos Mitsotakis told a banking conference in Athens on Monday, describing the move as a signal of the country's fiscal recovery.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Top House Democrat: Gabbard 'a national security threat' to US
item.description: Rep. Gregory Meeks (D-N.Y.), the ranking member on the House Foreign Affairs Committee, said Monday that former Democratic Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii), now a Republican and President-elect Trump’s pick for director of national intelligence, is “a national security threat” to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: India overtakes China as top source of international students at US colleges
item.description: India has overtaken China as the top source of international students for U.S. colleges, according to a report from Open Doors. For the 2023-2024 school year, India saw a massive 23 percent increase in the number of college students sent to America, up to 331,602.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: Trump Jr.: Biden administration trying to cause ‘World War 3’ by helping Ukraine
item.description: Donald Trump Jr., the eldest son of President-elect Trump, claimed the Biden administration is trying to cause “World War 3” by helping Ukraine in its war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Israeli strike kills Hezbollah spokesman in Beirut; at least 30 dead in Gaza airstrikes
item.description: A rare Israeli strike in central Beirut has killed the chief spokesman for the Hezbollah militant group. It was the latest in targeted killings of senior Hezbollah officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Abuse survivors urge the Vatican to globalize the zero-tolerance policy it approved in the US
item.description: Survivors of clergy sexual abuse are urging the Vatican to adopt the same zero-tolerance policy that it approved for the U.S. Catholic Church in 2002.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel strikes in the heart of Lebanon's capital
item.description: Israeli airstrikes targeted a neighborhood in the heart of Lebanon’s capital, slamming into an area of Beirut near the Parliament, several embassies and the U.N. headquarters.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: North Korean leader calls for expanding his nuclear forces in the face of alleged US threats
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korean leader Kim Jong Un renewed his call for a “limitless” expansion of his military nuclear program to counter U.S.-led threats in comments reported Monday that were his first direct criticism toward Washington since Donald Trump’s win in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An Israeli strike in Beirut kills Hezbollah's spokesman, while a strike in Gaza kills at least 30
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — A rare Israeli strike in central Beirut killed the Hezbollah militant group's chief spokesman on Sunday, while an Israeli strike in northern Gaza ’s Beit Lahiya killed at least 30 people, a hospital director there told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: FADI TAWIL, BASSEM MROUE and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia launched more than 200 missiles, drones at Ukrainian infrastructure: Zelensky
item.description: On Sunday, Russia launched a drone and missile attack on Ukraine in what’s being seen as the largest barrage in recent months, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Zelenskyy: Russia using Iranian weapons in 'massive' attack targeting energy infrastructure
item.description: Ukrainian forces say Russia launched over 200 drones and missiles against the nation's power and energy infrastructure this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexican president might be changing view on US as Trump win sends warning to ruling socialists
item.description: Following Trump’s victory, the Mexican government, led by President Claudia Sheinbaum, is considering reassessing some of its policies to navigate the anticipated challenges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In a meeting with Biden, China's Xi cautions US to 'make the wise choice' to keep relations stable
item.description: As U.S. President Joe Biden and China’s leader Xi Jinping met for the last time, Xi cautioned that a stable relationship between the two countries was critical to the 'future and destiny of humanity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: EU fines Meta over $840 million for ‘abusive practices’ favoring Facebook Marketplace
item.description: Meta received a fine of over $840 million on Wednesday for breaching the European Union’s antitrust rules as it relates to market domination after three years of legal proceedings.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: US health officials report 1st case of new form of mpox in a traveler
item.description: Health officials said Saturday they have confirmed the first U.S. case of a new form of mpox that was first seen in eastern Congo.
item.dc:creator: Mike Stobbe, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 2,000-year-old Roman road discovered by archaeologists in London
item.description: An ancient Roman road was recently discovered by archaeologists in London. The 2,000-year-old road was located beneath modern-day Old Kent Road.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel's warfare in Gaza 'consistent with genocide:' UN special committee
item.description: Israel's destruction of vital water, sanitation, and food systems has created a "lethal mix of crises," the report said.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Trump said to lift all military restrictions on Israel on 1st day in office according to reports
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump’s promise could lead to a 60-day cease-fire, allowing Israel to suspend hostilities until military support resumes under the new administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: India's capital introduces stricter anti-pollution measures as toxic smog hides Taj Mahal
item.description: To combat worsening air quality, India's government has banned non-essential construction and encouraged residents to avoid burning coal for heating.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Welsh government advised to create dog-free zones to combat racism
item.description: A climate change group has submitted a suggestion to Welsh government officials to create dog-free zones in an effort to combat racism, according to reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Moo Deng, Thailand's baby pygmy hippo, gets official song in 4 languages
item.description: In case you can’t get enough of the little pygmy hippo Moo Deng from Thailand, there's now an official song featuring the internet's favorite baby animal — released in four languages for her global fans.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: UN watchdog warns time to 'maneuver' on Iran's nuclear program is shrinking: report
item.description: Rafael Grossi, head of the International Atomic Energy Agency, the U.N. nuclear watchdog, says the window of opportunity to diplomatically engage with Iran is beginning to "shrink.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US government worker charged with leaking classified documents on Israel's plans to strike Iran
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A man who worked for the U.S. government has been charged with leaking classified information assessing Israel's earlier plans to attack Iran, according to court papers filed Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 1 dead, Supreme Court evacuated as explosions rattle Brazilian capital
item.description: The Brazilian Supreme Court was evacuated Wednesday following two explosions in its vicinity, stoking security concerns in the G20's leadup.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: President Joe Biden meets with Israel's Isaac Herzog
item.description: Joe Biden met with Israel President Isaac Herzog at the White House on Tuesday, which included a gift from Herzog to Biden.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Jury awards Abu Ghraib detainees $42 million, holds contractor responsible
item.description: ALEXANDRIA, Va. (AP) — A U.S. jury on Tuesday awarded $42 million to three former detainees of Iraq's notorious Abu Ghraib prison, holding a Virginia-based military contractor responsible for contributing to their torture and mistreatment two decades ago.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BARAKAT, Associated Press, NewsNation
